texts.label: text
texts.orig: Introduction
texts.text: Introduction

texts.label: text
texts.orig: OR*3.0*332 is the third patch released to address features of CPRS blood component and diagnostic test ordering changes. It also deals with several display issues, including how reports are formatted. The patch introduces several parameter changes or new parameters.
texts.text: OR*3.0*332 is the third patch released to address features of CPRS blood component and diagnostic test ordering changes. It also deals with several display issues, including how reports are formatted. The patch introduces several parameter changes or new parameters.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Functionality
texts.text: Functionality

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This patch makes changes to the Blood Bank report in CPRS, with several items that sites have requested. Some of these changes are hard-coded, while others are controlled by parameter. Patch OR*3.0*332 also adds some parameters and changes others to add some flexibility for sites using the VBECS dialog and Blood Bank report in CPRS.
texts.text: This patch makes changes to the Blood Bank report in CPRS, with several items that sites have requested. Some of these changes are hard-coded, while others are controlled by parameter. Patch OR*3.0*332 also adds some parameters and changes others to add some flexibility for sites using the VBECS dialog and Blood Bank report in CPRS.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Pre-Conditions to Installation
texts.text: Pre-Conditions to Installation

texts.orig: OR*3*332 requires the following:
texts.text: OR*3*332 requires the following:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Changes and Fixes to the Blood Bank Report
texts.text: Changes and Fixes to the Blood Bank Report

texts.label: text
texts.orig: – The Blood Bank report has been changed to display the Division name instead of the number in the last column of the Units Available section. This change was made to the display only. The printed version of the report will continue to print the Division number due to limited space on the line.
texts.text: – The Blood Bank report has been changed to display the Division name instead of the number in the last column of the Units Available section. This change was made to the display only. The printed version of the report will continue to print the Division number due to limited space on the line.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: - Blood Bank Report displays warning: “ABO Rh: 1^Not able to open port”.  This warning message comes back from VBECS when the VistALink is down. Test sites indicated that this message was confusing.
texts.text: - Blood Bank Report displays warning: “ABO Rh: 1^Not able to open port”.  This warning message comes back from VBECS when the VistALink is down. Test sites indicated that this message was confusing.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution: Developers changed CPRS to display the user-defined error message in the parameter OR VBECS ERROR MESSAGE.
texts.text: Resolution: Developers changed CPRS to display the user-defined error message in the parameter OR VBECS ERROR MESSAGE.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: - There are two places that display an error message when the VBECS VistALink port is down. Previously, the error message was inconsistent.
texts.text: - There are two places that display an error message when the VBECS VistALink port is down. Previously, the error message was inconsistent.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution: Now, the same text defined in the OR VBECS ERROR MESSAGE parameter is displayed in these two places:
texts.text: Resolution: Now, the same text defined in the OR VBECS ERROR MESSAGE parameter is displayed in these two places:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Patient Info Tab on the Blood Component and Diagnostic Test Order Form Dialog
texts.text: Patient Info Tab on the Blood Component and Diagnostic Test Order Form Dialog

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Blood Bank report
texts.text: The Blood Bank report

texts.label: text
texts.orig: – The AVAILABLE/ISSUED UNITS section of the Blood Bank report has been reformatted to make room for larger data fields and more columns.  A new column Product ID has been added to fix a problem with missing data when a patient had more than one Product with the same Unit ID. To give further flexibility,  a new parameter (OR VBECS AVAIL UNITS FORMAT) is included with this patch:
texts.text: – The AVAILABLE/ISSUED UNITS section of the Blood Bank report has been reformatted to make room for larger data fields and more columns.  A new column Product ID has been added to fix a problem with missing data when a patient had more than one Product with the same Unit ID. To give further flexibility,  a new parameter (OR VBECS AVAIL UNITS FORMAT) is included with this patch:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This parameter determines the format for how AVAILABLE/ISSUED UNITS display on the Blood Bank Report and the Patient Information screen on the Blood Bank Order Dialog.
texts.text: This parameter determines the format for how AVAILABLE/ISSUED UNITS display on the Blood Bank Report and the Patient Information screen on the Blood Bank Order Dialog.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The possible choices are:
texts.text: The possible choices are:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: 0 (zero) or horizontal
texts.text: 0 (zero) or horizontal

texts.label: text
texts.orig: 1 or vertical
texts.text: 1 or vertical

texts.label: text
texts.orig: 2 or split
texts.text: 2 or split

texts.label: text
texts.orig: What each choice will do to the display and printed report is explained below
texts.text: What each choice will do to the display and printed report is explained below

texts.label: text
texts.orig: 0:HORIZONTAL
texts.text: 0:HORIZONTAL

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Horizontal displays fields across the page, one line per date/time.
texts.text: Horizontal displays fields across the page, one line per date/time.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Horizontal printed reports split the line if it is longer than 79 characters as shown below.
texts.text: Horizontal printed reports split the line if it is longer than 79 characters as shown below.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: 1:VERTICAL
texts.text: 1:VERTICAL

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Vertical displays fields down the page in sections per date/time.
texts.text: Vertical displays fields down the page in sections per date/time.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Vertical printed report looks the same.
texts.text: The Vertical printed report looks the same.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: 2:SPLIT HORIZONTAL
texts.text: 2:SPLIT HORIZONTAL

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Split Horizontal keeps the Patient Information Screen the same as the HORIZONTAL format as shown below:
texts.text: Split Horizontal keeps the Patient Information Screen the same as the HORIZONTAL format as shown below:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Blood Bank Report splits the line if it is longer than 79 characters.
texts.text: The Blood Bank Report splits the line if it is longer than 79 characters.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Split Horizontal printed report splits the line if it is longer than 79 characters.
texts.text: The Split Horizontal printed report splits the line if it is longer than 79 characters.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The OR VBECS AVAIL UNITS FORMAT parameter was created to address issues found when printing the Blood Bank report in the horizontal format. The option of displaying/printing AVAILABLE/ISSUED UNITS vertically has been created.
texts.text: The OR VBECS AVAIL UNITS FORMAT parameter was created to address issues found when printing the Blood Bank report in the horizontal format. The option of displaying/printing AVAILABLE/ISSUED UNITS vertically has been created.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: As more fields with variable lengths are added to this report, the length of the line may go beyond the 80 character limit for printing. This is not an issue with the Windows display. The information from the Patient Information tab on the Blood Component and Diagnostic Test Order Form dialog cannot be printed, so no attempt has been made to split the line when it goes beyond 79 characters. It functions the same when either HORIZONTAL or SPLIT HORIZONTAL is selected.
texts.text: As more fields with variable lengths are added to this report, the length of the line may go beyond the 80 character limit for printing. This is not an issue with the Windows display. The information from the Patient Information tab on the Blood Component and Diagnostic Test Order Form dialog cannot be printed, so no attempt has been made to split the line when it goes beyond 79 characters. It functions the same when either HORIZONTAL or SPLIT HORIZONTAL is selected.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: For both HORIZONTAL display formats the length of the line expands or contracts depending on the column width of each field and the length of the longest line. Because of this it is possible, though not likely, that the line will not split even if SPLIT HORIZONTAL is selected.
texts.text: For both HORIZONTAL display formats the length of the line expands or contracts depending on the column width of each field and the length of the longest line. Because of this it is possible, though not likely, that the line will not split even if SPLIT HORIZONTAL is selected.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Each site can further customize the Blood Bank Report and Patient information display on the Blood Component and Diagnostic Test Order Form dialog by shortening the length of each line on the Horizontal formats by using the Location abbreviation instead of the Location Name. To do this, edit the parameter OR VBECS LOC ABBREV BB REPORT.
texts.text: Each site can further customize the Blood Bank Report and Patient information display on the Blood Component and Diagnostic Test Order Form dialog by shortening the length of each line on the Horizontal formats by using the Location abbreviation instead of the Location Name. To do this, edit the parameter OR VBECS LOC ABBREV BB REPORT.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This parameter is new with this patch and only affects the display of the report. The printed report will display the Location abbreviation due to space Limitations.
texts.text: This parameter is new with this patch and only affects the display of the report. The printed report will display the Location abbreviation due to space Limitations.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Blood Bank Report DAT Comment Is Missing the Last Line
texts.text: Blood Bank Report DAT Comment Is Missing the Last Line

texts.label: text
texts.orig: –Previously, the comment for a Direct Antiglobulin Test (DAT) was missing the last line of the comment.
texts.text: –Previously, the comment for a Direct Antiglobulin Test (DAT) was missing the last line of the comment.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution: This has been fixed so the entire comment is visible.
texts.text: Resolution: This has been fixed so the entire comment is visible.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Changes and Fixes to the Blood Order Dialog
texts.text: Changes and Fixes to the Blood Order Dialog

texts.label: text
texts.orig: – The field requested the ability to alphabetically sort the items in the Reason for Request field.
texts.text: – The field requested the ability to alphabetically sort the items in the Reason for Request field.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution: Developers added the new parameter OR VBECS REASON SORT ALPHA to enable sites to alphabetically sort the list of reasons in the Reason for Request field on the Blood Component and Diagnostic Test Order Form dialog.
texts.text: Resolution: Developers added the new parameter OR VBECS REASON SORT ALPHA to enable sites to alphabetically sort the list of reasons in the Reason for Request field on the Blood Component and Diagnostic Test Order Form dialog.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: – With the VBECS 1.6.1.0 update, VBECS sends CPRS a ‘no blood required’ flag for some surgical procedures.
texts.text: – With the VBECS 1.6.1.0 update, VBECS sends CPRS a ‘no blood required’ flag for some surgical procedures.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution: This patch adds the CPRS logic to recognize this flag so that CPRS will no longer require a specimen for those orders.
texts.text: Resolution: This patch adds the CPRS logic to recognize this flag so that CPRS will no longer require a specimen for those orders.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Note:
texts.text: Note:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Both patch OR*3.0*332 and VBECS 1.6.1.0 must be installed before users will see this functionality.
texts.text: Both patch OR*3.0*332 and VBECS 1.6.1.0 must be installed before users will see this functionality.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: – Previously, CPRS saved the specimen unique identifier (UID) with the order and transmitted it to VBECS even if the Date/Time Wanted was past the specimen’s expiration date or if the order was delayed, which could cause confusion.
texts.text: – Previously, CPRS saved the specimen unique identifier (UID) with the order and transmitted it to VBECS even if the Date/Time Wanted was past the specimen’s expiration date or if the order was delayed, which could cause confusion.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution: If the order’s Date/Time Wanted is past the specimen’s expiration date or if the order is a delayed order, CPRS will no longer save the specimen UID with the order nor transmit the specimen UID.
texts.text: Resolution: If the order’s Date/Time Wanted is past the specimen’s expiration date or if the order is a delayed order, CPRS will no longer save the specimen UID with the order nor transmit the specimen UID.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: CQ 19265: Display Reason for Request with All Child Orders
texts.text: CQ 19265: Display Reason for Request with All Child Orders

texts.label: text
texts.orig: –The Reason for Request had previously only been saved with component orders.
texts.text: –The Reason for Request had previously only been saved with component orders.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution: The Reason for Request will now be saved with all VBECS child orders, so it can be transmitted to VBECS. The Reason for Request displays in VBECS on the “VBECS – Order Details” screen.
texts.text: Resolution: The Reason for Request will now be saved with all VBECS child orders, so it can be transmitted to VBECS. The Reason for Request displays in VBECS on the “VBECS – Order Details” screen.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: –Developers discovered that component requests with the same date/time were overwriting each other and ended up missing on the report.
texts.text: –Developers discovered that component requests with the same date/time were overwriting each other and ended up missing on the report.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution: Developers corrected the problem and all of the data should now display.
texts.text: Resolution: Developers corrected the problem and all of the data should now display.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: – Previously, there was a problem with the Item Ordered field of lab child orders. Although it did not affect the ordering functions, the field was inadvertently being stuffed with the Orderable Item pointer, which was incorrect. Because the Orderable Item and the Item Ordered point to completely different files, a FileMan Inquiry of the order appeared to be incorrect. Item Ordered was meant to be used by developers to troubleshoot problems, not to build the order. Item Ordered stored which item was selected off the Write Orders menu. For child orders, this item the Item Ordered? should be empty because the child orders came from the parent order, not from a selection on the Write Orders menu.
texts.text: – Previously, there was a problem with the Item Ordered field of lab child orders. Although it did not affect the ordering functions, the field was inadvertently being stuffed with the Orderable Item pointer, which was incorrect. Because the Orderable Item and the Item Ordered point to completely different files, a FileMan Inquiry of the order appeared to be incorrect. Item Ordered was meant to be used by developers to troubleshoot problems, not to build the order. Item Ordered stored which item was selected off the Write Orders menu. For child orders, this item the Item Ordered? should be empty because the child orders came from the parent order, not from a selection on the Write Orders menu.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution: Developers added some code to ensure the Item Ordered field is empty for child orders, which is correct.
texts.text: Resolution: Developers added some code to ensure the Item Ordered field is empty for child orders, which is correct.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: CQ 20073, Remedy 457551
texts.text: CQ 20073, Remedy 457551

texts.label: text
texts.orig: – Add Division Level to OR VBECS REASON FOR REQUEST
texts.text: – Add Division Level to OR VBECS REASON FOR REQUEST

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Resolution: Developers added DIVISION level settings to the parameter OR VBECS REASON FOR REQUEST. This parameter is being updated in this patch.
texts.text: Resolution: Developers added DIVISION level settings to the parameter OR VBECS REASON FOR REQUEST. This parameter is being updated in this patch.

texts.text: Miscellaneous Fixes

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Unnecessary code removed from routine ORWLR that displays the Lab Cumulative in CPRS Reports.  This change may improve the speed that the report displays.
texts.text: Unnecessary code removed from routine ORWLR that displays the Lab Cumulative in CPRS Reports.  This change may improve the speed that the report displays.

texts.text: –New codes have been added to STATUS^ORMVBEC for compliance with HL7 standards; pre-existing codes will continue to be respected for backwards compatibility.
